info.title: Generic Maven rule for Mockito API break fix

parts.text: "Project @bval/ does not compile due to a breaking dependency update. Your goal is to generate a GENERIC, REUSABLE transformation rule - not a one-off patch - that can be applied to ANY Maven project affected by the same breaking change. Plan: 1) Identify the compilation errors in @bval/ and determine which API    (class/method/constructor/signature) changed in the dependency.    The new dependency API specification is available at /workspace/mockito-core-5.1.1-api.md and the Javadoc at /workspace/javadoc-mockito-core-5.1.1/    for reference when identifying replacement types, methods, or fields. 2) Characterize the breaking change abstractly:    - What was the old API pattern? (e.g., Foo.bar(String))    - What is the new API pattern? (e.g., Foo.bar(String, boolean))    - What structural transformation does this require at the call site?    Do NOT assume the fix is specific to this project - any project calling the old API will need the same transformation. 3) Generate a GENERIC Spoon transformation using the template in @/workspace/spoon-base-template/:    - Match the old API pattern structurally, not by project-specific class names.    - Use patterns to traverse all files and apply the fix wherever the old pattern appears.    - Parameterize by fully-qualified type names and method signatures from the dependency, NOT from the client.    - Save the transformation in /workspace/spoon-base-template/src/main/java/github/chains/Main.java.    - Use the Spoon API documentation located in folder /workspace/spoon-api-docs/ for reference. 4) Compile the transformation and verify it has no errors. 5) Apply the transformation to @bval/ and ensure both compilation errors and test errors are fixed. 6) Ensure generalizability: ensure the generated rule contains no hardcoded project-specific identifiers.    The rule must be applicable to other projects by simply changing the input source directory path."

**Considering code modifications**

I need to figure out if we should modify the code in the workspace. The repo root is showing as /workspace, but there’s no git repository there, so I'll need to inspect the directories. Using a task list sounds like a good idea, and I may need to explore for searching. Compiling transformations will be necessary as well. Since there’s no git repo, I can still edit, but I should document updates before making substantial changes. Let’s create a to-do list!
I’m locating the project, the Spoon template, and the dependency API docs first so I can derive the generic transformation from the actual breakage rather than guessing.

parts.text:
**Clarifying executable transformations**

I need to modify the transformation, possibly replacing the target and removing the cast. However, the output remains unchanged—maybe the renamed executable reference isn't pretty-printing due to unresolved invocation. In the pretty-printed output, I still see getArgumentAt, which is confusing. Perhaps something like getExecutable().setSimpleName doesn’t impact it. I might need to create a new executable reference using factory.Code().createExecutableReference. This means I should look up the Spoon API and search the documentation for CodeFactory.

I found the reason: changing the executable reference in place isn’t affecting the pretty-printed invocation. I’m switching to reconstructing the executable reference via Spoon’s factory API, which is the normal way to rewrite a call site.
